Staff Data Engineer

We're seeking an experienced Staff Data Engineer to lead our data platform's evolution and mentor

our growing engineering team. In this role, you'll architect scalable data pipelines, establish best

practices across data systems, and drive technical strategy for our infrastructure initiatives. You'll

work cross-functionally with product, analytics, and infrastructure teams while setting the technical

direction for data engineering at Nexus Analytics.

About the Company

Nexus Analytics is a fast-growing data infrastructure company that empowers enterprises to
transform raw data into actionable intelligence. Founded in 2019, we've built a platform that helps

Fortune 500 companies and ambitious startups alike manage, govern, and extract value from
petabyte-scale data ecosystems. Our mission is to democratize advanced data engineering practices

so every organization can make data-driven decisions in real time.

What You'll Own

1. Design and implement large-scale distributed data pipelines using Apache Spark and Airflow,

handling 100TB+ daily data volumes with sub-second latency requirements

2. Lead architectural decisions for data warehouse modernization efforts, evaluating trade-offs

between Snowflake, BigQuery, and Redshift for multi-tenant environments

3. Mentor and technically guide 4-6 mid-level and junior data engineers, conducting code reviews

and establishing coding standards across the data platform

4. Develop dbt transformations and data models that improve query performance by 40%+ while

maintaining governance and lineage tracking

5. Establish and own Kafka-based real-time data streaming architecture, designing schemas and

ensuring 99.99% uptime for mission-critical pipelines

6. Lead data modeling workshops with stakeholders to establish dimensional models, slowly-

changing dimensions, and fact table strategies

7. Champion internal tools and frameworks that reduce data pipeline development time by 50%,

including reusable Spark job templates and Airflow DAG factories

8. Define and enforce data quality standards using automated testing frameworks, reducing

incidents in production by 60%

at Nexus Analytics — Building the intelligent data backbone for modern enterprisesSan Francisco, CA (Hybrid) • Full-time • $240,000 - $310,000 USD annually 9. Collaborate with infrastructure and security teams to design data governance policies,

encryption strategies, and PII handling compliance

  Kafka   Python   SQL   BigQuery

Great Expectations   Kubernetes

Perks & Benefits

Competitive equity package (0.5-1.5% depending on level) with 4-year vest and 1-year cliff
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage with $0 employee premiums
Unlimited PTO plus 12 company holidays and 10 remote-work days annually
Annual professional development budget of $5,000 for conferences, courses, and certifications
$10,000 annual learning stipend for technical tools and subscriptions
Home office equipment allowance ($2,500 upfront) and monthly internet reimbursement
Parental leave (16 weeks paid for all parents) and flexible return-to-work arrangements
Mental wellness benefits including subsidized therapy and monthly wellness coaching

 Next Steps

Apply directly through our careers page at nexusanalytics.com/careers with your resume, GitHub

profile, and a brief cover letter describing your most complex data architecture project. Shortlisted

candidates will participate in a 30-minute screening call, followed by a technical design interview, a

code review exercise, and conversations with the hiring manager and team lead.
